quality.rationale: 多步微分形式外微分与勒维奇维塔张量运算结合度规行列式导出协变散度

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s8_8.10_b.md]Problem 8.10. [Note: This problem assumes a familiarity with the Cartan calculus, beyond the scope of most relativity texts. Succeeding problems do not depend on it.] In the language of differential forms the generalizedStokes' theorem is$$\int_{\Omega} \mathrm {d} \boldsymbol {\theta} = \int_{\partial \Omega} \boldsymbol {\theta}.$$To what does this theorem reduce, in the following cases(b) $\Omega$ is 4-dimensional, $\pmb{\theta} = \mathbf{f}^{\mu}\mathbf{d}^{3}\pmb{\Sigma}_{\mu}$ .
origin.solution: (b) $\pmb{\theta} = \mathbf{f}^{\mu}\mathbf{d}^{3}\pmb{\Sigma}_{\mu} = \mathbf{f}^{\mu}\frac{1}{3!}\varepsilon_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma}\widetilde{\mathbf{dx}}^{\alpha}\wedge \widetilde{\mathbf{dx}}^{\beta}\wedge \widetilde{\mathbf{dx}}^{\gamma}$$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {d} \boldsymbol {\theta} = \frac {1}{3 !} \left(\mathrm {f}^{\mu},_{\lambda} \varepsilon_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\lambda} + \mathrm {f}^{\mu} \mathrm {d} \varepsilon_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma}\right) \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\alpha} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \\\\ = \frac {1}{3 !} \left(\mathrm {f}^{\mu},_{\lambda} \varepsilon_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\lambda} + \mathrm {f}^{\mu} \frac {1}{2} \frac {\mathrm {g} ,_{\lambda}}{\mathrm {g}} \varepsilon_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\lambda}\right) \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\alpha} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \\\\ = \frac {1}{3 !} | \mathrm {g} |^{\- \frac {1}{2}} (| \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} f^{\mu}),_{\lambda}^{\varepsilon}_{\mu \alpha \beta \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\lambda} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\alpha} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \\\\ = \frac {\- 1}{3 ! \left| \mathrm {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(\left| \mathrm {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {f}^{\mu}\right)_{, \lambda} \varepsilon_{\mu a \beta \gamma} \varepsilon^{\lambda a \beta \gamma} | \mathrm {g} |^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{0} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{3} \\\\ = \frac {1}{3 ! \left| \mathbf {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}}} \left(\left| \mathbf {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathbf {f}^{\mu}\right)_{, \lambda} \delta_{\mu}^{\lambda} 3! \left| \mathbf {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {\mathbf {d x}}^{0} \wedge \widetilde {\mathbf {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathbf {d x}}^{2} \wedge \widetilde {\mathbf {d x}}^{3} \\\\ = \mathrm {f}_{; \mu}^{\mu} \left| \mathrm {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{0} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{1} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{2} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{3} . \\\\ \end{array}$$The theorem then reduces to$$\int_{\Omega} \nabla \cdot \mathbf {f} \left| \mathbf {g}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {d}^{4} \mathbf {x} = \int_{\partial \Omega} \mathbf {f}^{\mu} \mathrm {d}^{3} \Sigma_{\mu}.$$
